titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Progressive Black Associates Marketing Binder LSC.2465
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Kelly Besser, 2026.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 March 20.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-20 10:02:57 -0700.

did.abstract: Progressive Black Associates (P.B.A.) is a Los Angeles, California company which has undertaken the task of enhancing the study of Black History. P.B.A. is Black-owned and dedicated to the economic elevation of Black people. The collection was created in 1970 and contains a marketing binder for P.B.A. and its 1971 Black History Calendar with a Civil Rights typescript inserted into the binder's front pocket, circa 1981.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to the UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Langdon Manor Books, LLC, 2024.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Progressive Black Associates marketing binder (Collection 2465). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's Library Collections and Archives.

bioghist.head: Administrative/Biographical History
bioghist.p: "Progressive Black Associates (P.B.A.) is a Los Angeles, California company which has undertaken the task of enhancing the study of Black History. We are introducing the 1971 edition of our Black History Calendar, the second edition, with the hopes of generating an awareness of the role of Black people in the building of America. Progressive Black Associates is Black-owned and dedicated to the economic elevation of Black people. P.B.A. is endeavoring to become a viable Black business in the minority community."
Source: P.B.A. marketing binder Purpose, page 1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: This collection contains a 1970 marketing binder for Progressive Black Associates and its 1971 Black History Calendar. The 1970 calendar was mailed to the Los Angeles Urban League to establish a distribution agreement with them. This collection includes a Civil Rights typescript inserted into the binder's front pocket, circa 1981.

controlaccess.subject: African American business enterprises -- California -- Los Angeles

did.unittitle: 1971 Black History in calendar form buyer's specification sheet
Photographs of 1971 calendar portraits by painter Artis Lane
Artis Lane biography and P.B.A. business card
P.B.A. member photographs by Alan Gartenberg
Composite list of department stores and buyers
Resume vitae of Progressive Black Associates
